data.content:
I can see your project — it's an MSTest project targeting .NET 8. The express delivery test will fail since it expects `15.00m` but the method returns `10.00m`.

Let me start `dotnet watch test` so tests re-run automatically when you save changes:

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to use `dotnet watch test` for hot reload. This is a .NET 8 MSTest project. I can see one test that will fail: `CalculateShipping_ExpressDelivery_Returns15` expects 15.00m but the method returns 10.00m for "express".

Let me set up `dotnet watch test` for them. This will watch for file changes and re-run tests automatically.

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-22979129c31e48138f31c71163a2b196 && dotnet watch test --no-hot-reload 2>&1", "description": "Start dotnet watch test for hot reload", "mode": "async", "shellId": "dotnet-watch"}

data.reasoningText:
It seems the watcher isn't picking up the change. The "Fix the error to continue" message suggests it's waiting for a file change to retry. But let me verify the file was actually changed, and also check if implicit usings are enabled. The project doesn't have `<ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>`, so `System` needs to be explicitly imported.

Let me verify the file content was saved correctly.
